claim: Ghent to Amsterdam-Centraal by train takes around 2h30 with one change, with about 18 trains per day on the route.
quote: There are no direct services on this line, so you'll need to make 1 change along the way... You'll normally find around 18 trains per day running on this route.

claim: Eurostar links Brussels Midi and Amsterdam Centraal in about 1h52 with fares from €25 standard, €45 in Plus, with hourly or near-hourly frequency.
quote: Eurostar trains link Amsterdam Centraal & Brussels Midi every hour or two in 1h50... fares start at €25 in Standard, €45 in Plus (1st class) or €92 in Premier.

claim: Eurostar summer 2026 standard fares from Brussels to Amsterdam Centraal start at €29 one-way.
quote: Tickets in Standard class start from €29 for one way journeys on Eurostar to/from Brussels Midi/Zuid, Antwerpen Centraal, Liège Guillemins, Rotterdam Centraal, Schiphol Airport, Amsterdam Centraal.

claim: Utrecht is widely pitched as a quieter weekend alternative to Amsterdam — small, walkable and not yet swamped by tourists.
quote: Utrecht is a real weekend alternative to Amsterdam... it's small enough to vibe like a village, yet pumped with full-fat city-break variety. It is Amsterdam-lite, if you like; best of all, it's a city still undiscovered by hordes of tourists.

claim: Utrecht's Oudegracht canal runs on two levels, with old wharf cellars converted into cafes, shops and subterranean restaurants — the city's signature romantic feature.
quote: The downtown canals are markedly different to those found in other Dutch cities. Located one storey below the main street level, they're notable for their wharf cellars and quays, which once thronged with merchants but now host cool bars and subterranean restaurants.

claim: TEFAF Maastricht 2026 runs 14–19 March 2026 at MECC Maastricht.
quote: The upcoming edition of TEFAF Maastricht will take place from March 14 to 19, 2026.

claim: During TEFAF, Maastricht's restaurants run special menus and museums add programming, making the whole city function as a cultural-culinary stage; Limburg specialities like vlaai pies and zoervleis braise feature prominently.
quote: A visit to TEFAF is not only an aesthetic experience, but also a cultural and culinary outing—Maastricht is alive during this period with museums organizing special exhibitions, restaurants serving special menus, and the city breathing art.

claim: Average daily cost per person is roughly €218 in The Hague vs €191 in Rotterdam; Rotterdam tends to be pitched ahead of The Hague for couples for nightlife and romantic sights.
quote: The average daily cost (per person) in The Hague is €218, while the average daily cost in Rotterdam is €191... couples should spend more time in Rotterdam than The Hague, as you'll find plenty of romantic sights and fun activities in Rotterdam.

claim: The Hague pairs the Mauritshuis Golden-Age collection with Scheveningen beach walks, with the promenade reopening in 2026 after renovation.
quote: The beach of Scheveningen on the North Sea is wide and clean, and the beach promenade reopened in 2026 after a renovation with plenty of benches and waste bins.

claim: Mauritshuis is loaning Girl with a Pearl Earring, View of Delft and Diana and her Nymphs to the Rijksmuseum's major Vermeer retrospective opening February — a 2026 art-tourism pull for both Amsterdam and The Hague.
quote: Mauritshuis is loaning three Vermeers for the exhibition, including Diana and her Nymphs, View of Delft and Girl with a Pearl Earring.

claim: The 79th Holland Festival runs 3–28 June 2026 across Amsterdam venues including ITA, Nationale Opera & Ballet, Muziekgebouw and Carré, the country's biggest international performing-arts programme.
quote: The 79th edition of the Holland Festival takes place from 3 to 28 June 2026... performances held at various locations in Amsterdam, such as the Internationaal Theater Amsterdam (ITA), the Nationale Opera & Ballet, the Muziekgebouw aan 't IJ, Westergas, Carré, De Balie, and the Stedelijk Museum.

claim: Rotterdam's couple-oriented core is its modern architecture: MVRDV's Markthal (228 flats over 96 stalls and 8 restaurants) and Piet Blom's 1984 Cube Houses, plus the Het Witte Huis rooftop.
quote: Markthal was designed by MVRDV and completed in 2014... The horseshoe-shaped building houses 228 residential units right above 96 market stalls and restaurants on the ground floor.

claim: Kruisherenhotel Maastricht is a 60-room design hotel inside a 15th-century monastery, run by Oostwegel Collection / Design Hotels — the obvious romantic anchor in Maastricht.
quote: The Kruisherenhotel Maastricht is housed in the 15th-century Kruisheren monastery. It was transformed into a 60-room luxury design hotel, officially opening its doors in 2005.

claim: Pillows Maurits at the Park (Amsterdam) is an 88-room 5-star boutique in a 1908 building, awarded two MICHELIN Keys in 2025 — a strong couple's pick away from the centre crush.
quote: The newly renovated building, dating back to 1908, is now a luxury five-star hotel, boasting of 88 luxury rooms... In 2025, it was awarded Two MICHELIN Keys.

claim: De Belhamel in the Jordaan is the canonical canal-side date dinner: art-nouveau room, French-Italian menu, candlelit waterside terrace at the junction of two canals.
quote: Tucked away at the junction of two canals, De Belhamel offers classic French-Italian cuisine in an art nouveau setting with candlelit tables and a waterside terrace, ideal for sunset dinners.
